evals.prompt: Sort today's time. I have TimeCamp activities, calendar events, and maybe some Telus calls. Just give me the full breakdown by matter and category.
evals.expected_output: A sorted timeline table showing all activities with times, durations, task/category assignments, confidence levels, and a summary of hours by matter and category

evals.prompt: Sort time for April 7th. I know there's some overlap between my calendar (I have a 2pm client call with Smith) and Telus data (call log shows a call at 2:15pm). Also had LEAP open for the Jones file at that same time. Figure out what actually happened.
evals.expected_output: Reconciled timeline that resolves the conflicting signals, showing how the overlapping data was merged and what task it was assigned to, with source attribution for each activity

evals.prompt: I sorted last Tuesday (April 1st) and changed a few activities. Now re-sort that day with fresh data from all sources. What changed, and did my old corrections stick?
evals.expected_output: A report showing the re-sorted day, noting which corrections from the previous sort were re-applied vs. skipped, and highlighting any new activities or changes in source data

evals.prompt: How much time did I spend on the Smith v Jones file (L3948) in the past week? Break it down by day.
evals.expected_output: A summary by day showing hours on the Smith file, calculated from stored sorted days across the week

evals.prompt: Sort time for March 15th, but TimeCamp and Google Calendar are unavailable. Use whatever data I can get from Telus Connect CSV at /tmp/telus_march.csv.
evals.expected_output: Sorted timeline using only Telus data, with clear notes about which sources failed and what data is missing (no TimeCamp screen activities, no calendar meetings)
